88: func New(conf *Config) (*Worker, error) {

90: 	metric.InitializeWebsocketCollectors(conf.PrometheusRegisterer)
91: 	metric.InitializeClusterClientCollectors(conf.PrometheusRegisterer)

93: 	w := &Worker{
94: 		conf:                   conf,
95: 		logger:                 conf.Logger.Named("worker"),
96: 		started:                ua.NewBool(false),
97: 		controllerStatusConn:   new(atomic.Value),
98: 		everAuthenticated:      ua.NewBool(false),
99: 		lastStatusSuccess:      new(atomic.Value),
100: 		controllerResolver:     new(atomic.Value),
101: 		controllerSessionConn:  new(atomic.Value),
102: 		sessionInfoMap:         new(sync.Map),
103: 		controllerMultihopConn: new(atomic.Value),
104: 		tags:                   new(atomic.Value),
105: 		updateTags:             ua.NewBool(false),
106: 		nonceFn:                base62.Random,
107: 	}
108: 
109: 	w.lastStatusSuccess.Store((*LastStatusInformation)(nil))
110: 	w.controllerResolver.Store((*manual.Resolver)(nil))
111: 
112: 	if conf.RawConfig.Worker == nil {
113: 		conf.RawConfig.Worker = new(config.Worker)
114: 	}
115: 
116: 	w.ParseAndStoreTags(conf.RawConfig.Worker.Tags)
117: 
118: 	if conf.SecureRandomReader == nil {
119: 		conf.SecureRandomReader = rand.Reader
120: 	}
121: 
122: 	var err error
123: 	if conf.RawConfig.Worker.Name, err = w.conf.RawConfig.Worker.InitNameIfEmpty(); err != nil {
124: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error auto-generating worker name: %w", err)
125: 	}
126: 
127: 	if !conf.RawConfig.DisableMlock {
128: 		// Ensure our memory usage is locked into physical RAM
129: 		if err := mlock.LockMemory(); err != nil {
130: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf(
131: 				"Failed to lock memory: %v\n\n"+
132: 					"This usually means that the mlock syscall is not available.\n"+
133: 					"Boundary uses mlock to prevent memory from being swapped to\n"+
134: 					"disk. This requires root privileges as well as a machine\n"+
135: 					"that supports mlock. Please enable mlock on your system or\n"+
136: 					"disable Boundary from using it. To disable Boundary from using it,\n"+
137: 					"set the `disable_mlock` configuration option in your configuration\n"+
138: 					"file.",
139: 				err)
140: 		}
141: 	}
142: 
143: 	var listenerCount int
144: 	for i := range conf.Listeners {
145: 		l := conf.Listeners[i]
146: 		if l == nil || l.Config == nil || l.Config.Purpose == nil {
147: 			continue
148: 		}
149: 		if len(l.Config.Purpose) != 1 {
150: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("found listener with multiple purposes %q", strings.Join(l.Config.Purpose, ","))
151: 		}
152: 		switch l.Config.Purpose[0] {
153: 		case "proxy":
154: 			if w.proxyListener == nil {
155: 				w.proxyListener = l
156: 			}
157: 			listenerCount++
158: 		}
159: 	}
160: 	if listenerCount != 1 {
161: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("exactly one proxy listener is required")
162: 	}
163: 
164: 	w.NodeeFileStorage, err = nodeefile.NewFileStorage(w.baseContext,
165: 		nodeefile.WithBaseDirectory(w.conf.RawConfig.Worker.AuthStoragePath))
166: 	if err != nil {
167: 		return nil, err
168: 	}
169: 
170: 	return w, nil
171: }

173: func (w *Worker) Start() error {
174: 	const op = "worker.(Worker).Start"
175: 
176: 	w.baseContext, w.baseCancel = context.WithCancel(context.Background())
177: 
178: 	if w.started.Load() {
179: 		event.WriteSysEvent(w.baseContext, op, "already started, skipping")
180: 		return nil
181: 	}
182: 
183: 	scheme := strconv.FormatInt(time.Now().UnixNano(), 36)
184: 	controllerResolver := manual.NewBuilderWithScheme(scheme)
185: 	w.controllerResolver.Store(controllerResolver)
186: 
187: 	if err := w.startListeners(); err != nil {
188: 		return fmt.Errorf("error starting worker listeners: %w", err)
189: 	}
190: 
191: 	nodeCreds, err := types.NewNodeCredentials(w.baseContext, w.NodeeFileStorage, nodeenrollment.WithWrapper(w.conf.WorkerAuthStorageKms))

194: 	}

197: 	if err != nil {

199: 	}

201: 	if err != nil {
202: 		return fmt.Errorf("error marshaling fetch credentials request: %w", err)
203: 	}

207: 	}

210: 		return fmt.Errorf("error deriving key id: %w", err)
211: 	}
212: 
213: 	if err := w.startControllerConnections(); err != nil {
214: 		return fmt.Errorf("error making controller connections: %w", err)
215: 	}
216: 
217: 	w.tickerWg.Add(1)
218: 	go func() {
219: 		defer w.tickerWg.Done()
220: 		w.startStatusTicking(w.baseContext)
221: 	}()
222: 
223: 	w.workerStartTime = time.Now()
224: 	w.started.Store(true)
225: 
226: 	return nil
227: }

229: // Shutdown shuts down the workers. skipListeners can be used to not stop
230: // listeners, useful for tests if we want to stop and start a worker. In order
231: // to create new listeners we'd have to migrate listener setup logic here --
232: // doable, but work for later.
233: func (w *Worker) Shutdown() error {
234: 	const op = "worker.(Worker).Shutdown"
235: 	if !w.started.Load() {
236: 		event.WriteSysEvent(w.baseContext, op, "already shut down, skipping")
237: 		return nil
238: 	}
239: 
240: 	// Stop listeners first to prevent new connections to the
241: 	// controller.
242: 	defer w.started.Store(false)
243: 	w.Resolver().UpdateState(resolver.State{Addresses: []resolver.Address{}})
244: 	w.baseCancel()
245: 
246: 	if err := w.stopServersAndListeners(); err != nil {
247: 		return fmt.Errorf("error stopping worker servers and listeners: %w", err)
248: 	}
249: 
250: 	// Shut down all connections.
251: 	w.cleanupConnections(w.baseContext, true)
252: 
253: 	// Wait for next status request to succeed. Don't wait too long;
254: 	// wrap the base context in a timeout equal to our status grace
255: 	// period.
256: 	waitStatusStart := time.Now()
257: 	nextStatusCtx, nextStatusCancel := context.WithTimeout(w.baseContext, w.conf.StatusGracePeriodDuration)
258: 	defer nextStatusCancel()
259: 	for {
260: 		if err := nextStatusCtx.Err(); err != nil {
261: 			event.WriteError(w.baseContext, op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("error waiting for next status report to controller"))
262: 			break
263: 		}
264: 
265: 		if w.lastSuccessfulStatusTime().Sub(waitStatusStart) > 0 {
266: 			break
267: 		}
268: 
269: 		time.Sleep(time.Second)
270: 	}
271: 
272: 	// Proceed with remainder of shutdown.
273: 	w.baseCancel()
274: 	w.Resolver().UpdateState(resolver.State{Addresses: []resolver.Address{}})
275: 
276: 	w.started.Store(false)
277: 	w.tickerWg.Wait()
278: 	if w.conf.Eventer != nil {
279: 		if err := w.conf.Eventer.FlushNodes(context.Background()); err != nil {
280: 			return fmt.Errorf("error flushing worker eventer nodes: %w", err)
281: 		}
282: 	}
283: 
284: 	return nil
285: }

295: func (w *Worker) ParseAndStoreTags(incoming map[string][]string) {
296: 	if len(incoming) == 0 {
297: 		w.tags.Store([]*servers.TagPair{})
298: 		return
299: 	}
300: 	tags := []*servers.TagPair{}
301: 	for k, vals := range incoming {
302: 		for _, v := range vals {
303: 			tags = append(tags, &servers.TagPair{
304: 				Key:   k,
305: 				Value: v,
306: 			})
307: 		}
308: 	}
309: 	w.tags.Store(tags)
310: 	w.updateTags.Store(true)
311: }

313: func (w *Worker) ControllerSessionConn() (pbs.SessionServiceClient, error) {
314: 	rawConn := w.controllerSessionConn.Load()
315: 	if rawConn == nil {
316: 		return nil, errors.New("unable to load controller session service connection")
317: 	}
318: 	sessClient, ok := rawConn.(pbs.SessionServiceClient)
319: 	if !ok {
320: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid session client %T", rawConn)
321: 	}
322: 	if sessClient == nil {
323: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("session client is nil")
324: 	}
325: 
326: 	return sessClient, nil
327: }

329: func (w *Worker) getSessionTls(hello *tls.ClientHelloInfo) (*tls.Config, error) {
330: 	const op = "worker.(Worker).getSessionTls"
331: 	ctx := w.baseContext
332: 	var sessionId string
333: 	switch {
334: 	case strings.HasPrefix(hello.ServerName, globals.SessionPrefix):
335: 		sessionId = hello.ServerName
336: 	default:
337: 		for _, proto := range hello.SupportedProtos {
338: 			if strings.HasPrefix(proto, globals.SessionPrefix) {
339: 				sessionId = proto
340: 				break
341: 			}
342: 		}
343: 	}
344: 
345: 	if sessionId == "" {
346: 		event.WriteSysEvent(ctx, op, "session_id not found in either SNI or ALPN protos", "server_name", hello.ServerName, "alpn_protos", hello.SupportedProtos)
347: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("could not find session ID in SNI or ALPN protos")
348: 	}
349: 
350: 	conn, err := w.ControllerSessionConn()
351: 	if err != nil {
352: 		event.WriteError(ctx, op, err, event.WithInfo("failed to create controller session client"))
353: 	}
354: 
355: 	timeoutContext, cancel := context.WithTimeout(w.baseContext, session.ValidateSessionTimeout)
356: 	defer cancel()
357: 
358: 	resp, err := conn.LookupSession(timeoutContext, &pbs.LookupSessionRequest{
359: 		ServerId:  w.conf.RawConfig.Worker.Name,
360: 		SessionId: sessionId,
361: 	})
362: 	if err != nil {
363: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error validating session: %w", err)
364: 	}
365: 
366: 	if resp.GetExpiration().AsTime().Before(time.Now()) {
367: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("session is expired")
368: 	}
369: 
370: 	parsedCert, err := x509.ParseCertificate(resp.GetAuthorization().Certificate)
371: 	if err != nil {
372: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error parsing session certificate: %w", err)
373: 	}
374: 
375: 	certPool := x509.NewCertPool()
376: 	certPool.AddCert(parsedCert)
377: 
378: 	tlsConf := &tls.Config{
379: 		Certificates: []tls.Certificate{
380: 			{
381: 				Certificate: [][]byte{resp.GetAuthorization().Certificate},
382: 				PrivateKey:  ed25519.PrivateKey(resp.GetAuthorization().PrivateKey),
383: 				Leaf:        parsedCert,
384: 			},
385: 		},
386: 		NextProtos: []string{"http/1.1"},
387: 		MinVersion: tls.VersionTLS13,
388: 
389: 		// These two are set this way so we can make use of VerifyConnection,
390: 		// which we set on this TLS config below. We are not skipping
391: 		// verification!
392: 		ClientAuth:         tls.RequireAnyClientCert,
393: 		InsecureSkipVerify: true,
394: 	}
395: 
396: 	// We disable normal DNS SAN behavior as we don't rely on DNS or IP
397: 	// addresses for security and want to avoid issues with including localhost
398: 	// etc.
399: 	verifyOpts := x509.VerifyOptions{
400: 		DNSName: sessionId,
401: 		Roots:   certPool,
402: 		KeyUsages: []x509.ExtKeyUsage{
403: 			x509.ExtKeyUsageClientAuth,
404: 			x509.ExtKeyUsageServerAuth,
405: 		},
406: 	}
407: 	if w.TestOverrideX509VerifyCertPool != nil {
408: 		verifyOpts.Roots = w.TestOverrideX509VerifyCertPool
409: 	}
410: 	if w.TestOverrideX509VerifyDnsName != "" {
411: 		verifyOpts.DNSName = w.TestOverrideX509VerifyDnsName
412: 	}
413: 	tlsConf.VerifyConnection = func(cs tls.ConnectionState) error {
414: 		// Go will not run this without at least one peer certificate, but
415: 		// doesn't hurt to check
416: 		if len(cs.PeerCertificates) == 0 {
417: 			return errors.New("no peer certificates provided")
418: 		}
419: 		_, err := cs.PeerCertificates[0].Verify(verifyOpts)
420: 		return err
421: 	}
422: 
423: 	si := &session.Info{
424: 		Id:                    resp.GetAuthorization().GetSessionId(),
425: 		SessionTls:            tlsConf,
426: 		LookupSessionResponse: resp,
427: 		Status:                resp.GetStatus(),
428: 		ConnInfoMap:           make(map[string]*session.ConnInfo),
429: 	}
430: 	// TODO: Periodically clean this up. We can't rely on things in here but
431: 	// not in cancellation because they could be on the way to being
432: 	// established. However, since cert lifetimes are short, we can simply range
433: 	// through and remove values that are expired.
434: 	actualSiRaw, loaded := w.sessionInfoMap.LoadOrStore(sessionId, si)
435: 	if loaded {
436: 		// Update the response to the latest
437: 		actualSi := actualSiRaw.(*session.Info)
438: 		actualSi.Lock()
439: 		actualSi.LookupSessionResponse = resp
440: 		actualSi.Unlock()
441: 	}
442: 
443: 	return tlsConf, nil
444: }

29: func (w *Worker) startListeners() error {
30: 	const op = "worker.(Worker).startListeners"
31: 
32: 	e := event.SysEventer()
33: 	if e == nil {
34: 		return fmt.Errorf("%s: sys eventer not initialized", op)
35: 	}
36: 	logger, err := e.StandardLogger(w.baseContext, "worker.listeners: ", event.ErrorType)
37: 	if err != nil {
38: 		return fmt.Errorf("%s: unable to initialize std logger: %w", op, err)
39: 	}
40: 	if w.proxyListener == nil {
41: 		return fmt.Errorf("%s: nil proxy listener", op)
42: 	}
43: 
44: 	workerServer, err := w.configureForWorker(w.proxyListener, logger)
45: 	if err != nil {
46: 		return fmt.Errorf("%s: failed to configure for worker: %w", op, err)
47: 	}
48: 
49: 	workerServer()
50: 
51: 	return nil
52: }
53: 
54: func (w *Worker) configureForWorker(ln *base.ServerListener, logger *log.Logger) (func(), error) {
55: 	const op = "worker.configureForWorker"
56: 
57: 	handler, err := w.handler(HandlerProperties{ListenerConfig: ln.Config})
58: 	if err != nil {
59: 		return nil, err
60: 	}
61: 
62: 	cancelCtx := w.baseContext
63: 	httpServer := &http.Server{
64: 		Handler:           handler,
65: 		ReadHeaderTimeout: 10 * time.Second,
66: 		ReadTimeout:       30 * time.Second,
67: 		ErrorLog:          logger,
68: 		BaseContext: func(net.Listener) context.Context {
69: 			return cancelCtx
70: 		},
71: 	}
72: 	ln.HTTPServer = httpServer
73: 
74: 	if ln.Config.HTTPReadHeaderTimeout > 0 {
75: 		httpServer.ReadHeaderTimeout = ln.Config.HTTPReadHeaderTimeout
76: 	}
77: 	if ln.Config.HTTPReadTimeout > 0 {
78: 		httpServer.ReadTimeout = ln.Config.HTTPReadTimeout
79: 	}
80: 	if ln.Config.HTTPWriteTimeout > 0 {
81: 		httpServer.WriteTimeout = ln.Config.HTTPWriteTimeout
82: 	}
83: 	if ln.Config.HTTPIdleTimeout > 0 {
84: 		httpServer.IdleTimeout = ln.Config.HTTPIdleTimeout
85: 	}
86: 
87: 	fetchCredsFn := func(
88: 		ctx context.Context,
89: 		_ nodee.Storage,
90: 		req *types.FetchNodeCredentialsRequest,
91: 		_ ...nodee.Option,
92: 	) (*types.FetchNodeCredentialsResponse, error) {
93: 		client := w.controllerMultihopConn.Load()
94: 		if client == nil {
95: 			return nil, temperror.New(errors.New("error fetching controller connection, client is nil"))
96: 		}
97: 		multihopClient, ok := client.(multihop.MultihopServiceClient)
98: 		if !ok {
99: 			return nil, temperror.New(errors.New("client could not be understood as a multihop service client"))
100: 		}
101: 		// log.Println("proxying fetch node credentials request")
102: 		return multihopClient.FetchNodeCredentials(ctx, req)
103: 	}
104: 
105: 	generateServerCertificatesFn := func(
106: 		ctx context.Context,
107: 		_ nodee.Storage,
108: 		req *types.GenerateServerCertificatesRequest,
109: 		_ ...nodee.Option,
110: 	) (*types.GenerateServerCertificatesResponse, error) {
111: 		client := w.controllerMultihopConn.Load()
112: 		if client == nil {
113: 			return nil, temperror.New(errors.New("error fetching controller connection, client is nil"))
114: 		}
115: 		multihopClient, ok := client.(multihop.MultihopServiceClient)
116: 		if !ok {
117: 			return nil, temperror.New(errors.New("client could not be understood as a multihop service client"))
118: 		}
119: 		// log.Println("proxying generate server cert request")
120: 		return multihopClient.GenerateServerCertificates(ctx, req)
121: 	}
122: 
123: 	interceptingListener, err := protocol.NewInterceptingListener(
124: 		&protocol.InterceptingListenerConfiguration{
125: 			Context:      w.baseContext,
126: 			Storage:      w.NodeeFileStorage,
127: 			BaseListener: ln.ProxyListener,
128: 			BaseTlsConfiguration: &tls.Config{
129: 				GetConfigForClient: w.getSessionTls,
130: 			},
131: 			FetchCredsFunc:                 fetchCredsFn,
132: 			GenerateServerCertificatesFunc: generateServerCertificatesFn,
133: 		})
134: 	if err != nil {
135: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error instantiating node auth listener: %w", err)
136: 	}
137: 
138: 	w.nodeeSplitListener = splitlistener.New(interceptingListener)
139: 
140: 	downstreamServer := grpc.NewServer(
141: 		grpc.MaxRecvMsgSize(math.MaxInt32),
142: 		grpc.MaxSendMsgSize(math.MaxInt32),
143: 	)
144: 	multihopService, err := handlers.NewMultihopServiceServer(
145: 		w.NodeeFileStorage,
146: 		false,
147: 		w.controllerMultihopConn,
148: 	)
149: 	if err != nil {
150: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: error creating multihop service handler: %w", op, err)
151: 	}
152: 	multihop.RegisterMultihopServiceServer(downstreamServer, multihopService)
153: 	statusSessionService := NewWorkerProxyServiceServer(w.controllerStatusConn, w.controllerSessionConn)
154: 	pbs.RegisterServerCoordinationServiceServer(downstreamServer, statusSessionService)
155: 	pbs.RegisterSessionServiceServer(downstreamServer, statusSessionService)
156: 
157: 	ln.GrpcServer = downstreamServer
158: 
159: 	return func() {
160: 		go w.nodeeSplitListener.Start()
161: 		go httpServer.Serve(w.nodeeSplitListener.OtherListener())
162: 		go ln.GrpcServer.Serve(w.nodeeSplitListener.NodeEnrollmentListener())
163: 	}, nil
164: }
165: 
166: func (w *Worker) stopServersAndListeners() error {
167: 	var mg multierror.Group
168: 	mg.Go(w.stopHttpServer)
169: 	mg.Go(w.stopClusterGrpcServer)
170: 
171: 	// FIXME (jeff): For some reason, unlike the controller, the grpc server
172: 	// really likes to hang on closing. Maybe because it's never served a
173: 	// connection? This is a workaround to force it until I can dig in.
174: 	var cancel context.CancelFunc
175: 	if w.nodeeSplitListener != nil {
176: 		var ctx context.Context
177: 		ctx, cancel = context.WithTimeout(w.baseContext, 2*time.Second)
178: 		go func() {
179: 			<-ctx.Done()
180: 			w.nodeeSplitListener.Stop()
181: 			cancel()
182: 		}()
183: 	}
184: 
185: 	stopErrors := mg.Wait()
186: 
187: 	if w.nodeeSplitListener != nil {
188: 		cancel()
189: 		err := w.nodeeSplitListener.Stop()
190: 		if err != nil {
191: 			stopErrors = multierror.Append(stopErrors, err)
192: 		}
193: 	}
194: 
195: 	err := w.stopAnyListeners()
196: 	if err != nil {
197: 		stopErrors = multierror.Append(stopErrors, err)
198: 	}
199: 
200: 	return stopErrors.ErrorOrNil()
201: }
202: 
203: func (w *Worker) stopHttpServer() error {
204: 	if w.proxyListener == nil {
205: 		return nil
206: 	}
207: 
208: 	if w.proxyListener.HTTPServer == nil {
209: 		return nil
210: 	}
211: 
212: 	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(w.baseContext, w.proxyListener.Config.MaxRequestDuration)
213: 	w.proxyListener.HTTPServer.Shutdown(ctx)
214: 	cancel()
215: 
216: 	return nil
217: }
218: 
219: func (w *Worker) stopClusterGrpcServer() error {
220: 	if w.proxyListener == nil {
221: 		return nil
222: 	}
223: 	if w.proxyListener.GrpcServer == nil {
224: 		return nil
225: 	}
226: 
227: 	w.proxyListener.GrpcServer.GracefulStop()
228: 	return nil
229: }
230: 
231: // stopAnyListeners does a final once over the known
232: // listeners to make sure we didn't miss any;
233: // expected to run at the end of stopServersAndListeners.
234: func (w *Worker) stopAnyListeners() error {
235: 	if w.proxyListener == nil {
236: 		return nil
237: 	}
238: 	var closeErrors *multierror.Error
239: 	var err error
240: 	if w.nodeeSplitListener != nil {
241: 		err = w.nodeeSplitListener.Stop()
242: 	} else if w.proxyListener.ProxyListener != nil {
243: 		err = w.proxyListener.ProxyListener.Close()
244: 	}
245: 	err = listenerCloseErrorCheck("proxy", err)
246: 	if err != nil {
247: 		closeErrors = multierror.Append(closeErrors, err)
248: 	}
249: 
250: 	return closeErrors.ErrorOrNil()
251: }
252: 
253: // listenerCloseErrorCheck does some validation on an error returned
254: // by a net.Listener's Close function, and ignores a few cases
255: // where we don't actually want an error to be returned.
256: func listenerCloseErrorCheck(lnType string, err error) error {
257: 	if errors.Is(err, net.ErrClosed) {
258: 		// Ignore net.ErrClosed - The listener was already closed,
259: 		// so there's nothing else to do.
260: 		return nil
261: 	}
262: 	if _, ok := err.(*os.PathError); ok && lnType == "unix" {
263: 		// The underlying rmListener probably tried to remove
264: 		// the file but it didn't exist, ignore the error;
265: 		// this is a conflict between rmListener and the
266: 		// default Go behavior of removing auto-vivified
267: 		// Unix domain sockets.
268: 		return nil
269: 	}
270: 
271: 	return err
272: }

75:   Start a server (controller, worker, or both) with a configuration file:

137: func (c *Command) Run(args []string) int {
138: 	c.CombineLogs = c.flagCombineLogs
139: 
140: 	if result := c.ParseFlagsAndConfig(args); result > 0 {
141: 		return result
142: 	}
143: 
144: 	if err := c.SetupLogging(c.flagLogLevel, c.flagLogFormat, c.Config.LogLevel, c.Config.LogFormat); err != nil {
145: 		c.UI.Error(err.Error())
146: 		return base.CommandUserError
147: 	}
148: 
149: 	var serverNames []string
150: 	if c.Config.Controller != nil {
151: 		if _, err := c.Config.Controller.InitNameIfEmpty(); err != nil {
152: 			c.UI.Error(err.Error())
153: 			return base.CommandUserError
154: 		}
155: 		serverNames = append(serverNames, c.Config.Controller.Name)
156: 	}
157: 	if c.Config.Worker != nil {
158: 		if _, err := c.Config.Worker.InitNameIfEmpty(); err != nil {
159: 			c.UI.Error(err.Error())
160: 			return base.CommandUserError
161: 		}
162: 		serverNames = append(serverNames, c.Config.Worker.Name)
163: 	}
164: 
165: 	if err := c.SetupEventing(c.Logger,
166: 		c.StderrLock,
167: 		strings.Join(serverNames, "/"),
168: 		base.WithEventerConfig(c.Config.Eventing),
169: 		base.WithEventGating(true)); err != nil {
170: 		c.UI.Error(err.Error())
171: 		return base.CommandUserError
172: 	}
173: 
174: 	// Initialize status grace period (0 denotes using env or default
175: 	// here)
176: 	c.SetStatusGracePeriodDuration(0)
177: 
178: 	base.StartMemProfiler(c.Context)
179: 
180: 	if err := c.SetupKMSes(c.Context, c.UI, c.Config); err != nil {
181: 		c.UI.Error(err.Error())
182: 		return base.CommandUserError
183: 	}
184: 	if c.Config.Controller != nil {
185: 		if c.RootKms == nil {
186: 			c.UI.Error("Root KMS not found after parsing KMS blocks")
187: 			return base.CommandUserError
188: 		}
189: 	}
190: 
191: 	if c.Config.DefaultMaxRequestDuration != 0 {
192: 		globals.DefaultMaxRequestDuration = c.Config.DefaultMaxRequestDuration
193: 	}
194: 
195: 	// If mlockall(2) isn't supported, show a warning. We disable this in dev
196: 	// because it is quite scary to see when first using Boundary. We also disable
197: 	// this if the user has explicitly disabled mlock in configuration.
198: 	if !c.Config.DisableMlock && !mlock.Supported() {
199: 		c.UI.Warn(base.WrapAtLength(
200: 			"WARNING! mlock is not supported on this system! An mlockall(2)-like " +
201: 				"syscall to prevent memory from being swapped to disk is not " +
202: 				"supported on this system. For better security, only run Boundary on " +
203: 				"systems where this call is supported. If you are running Boundary" +
204: 				"in a Docker container, provide the IPC_LOCK cap to the container."))
205: 	}
206: 
207: 	// Perform controller-specific listener checks here before setup
208: 	var clusterAddr string
209: 	var foundApi bool
210: 	var foundProxy bool
211: 	for _, lnConfig := range c.Config.Listeners {
212: 		switch len(lnConfig.Purpose) {
213: 		case 0:
214: 			c.UI.Error("Listener specified without a purpose")
215: 			return base.CommandUserError
216: 
217: 		case 1:
218: 			purpose := lnConfig.Purpose[0]
219: 			switch purpose {
220: 			case "cluster":
221: 				clusterAddr = lnConfig.Address
222: 				if clusterAddr == "" {
223: 					clusterAddr = "127.0.0.1:9201"
224: 					lnConfig.Address = clusterAddr
225: 				}
226: 			case "api":
227: 				foundApi = true
228: 			case "proxy":
229: 				foundProxy = true
230: 				if lnConfig.Address == "" {
231: 					lnConfig.Address = "127.0.0.1:9202"
232: 				}
233: 			case "ops":
234: 			default:
235: 				c.UI.Error(fmt.Sprintf("Unknown listener purpose %q", lnConfig.Purpose[0]))
236: 				return base.CommandUserError
237: 			}
238: 
239: 		default:
240: 			c.UI.Error("Specifying a listener with more than one purpose is not supported")
241: 			return base.CommandUserError
242: 		}
243: 	}
244: 	if c.Config.Controller != nil {
245: 		if !foundApi {
246: 			c.UI.Error(`Config activates controller but no listener with "api" purpose found`)
247: 			return base.CommandUserError
248: 		}
249: 		if clusterAddr == "" {
250: 			c.UI.Error(`Config activates controller but no listener with "cluster" purpose found`)
251: 			return base.CommandUserError
252: 		}
253: 		if err := c.SetupControllerPublicClusterAddress(c.Config, ""); err != nil {
254: 			c.UI.Error(err.Error())
255: 			return base.CommandUserError
256: 		}
257: 		c.InfoKeys = append(c.InfoKeys, "controller public cluster addr")
258: 		c.Info["controller public cluster addr"] = c.Config.Controller.PublicClusterAddr
259: 	}
260: 
261: 	if c.Config.Worker != nil {
262: 		if !foundProxy {
263: 			c.UI.Error(`Config activates worker but no listener with "proxy" purpose found`)
264: 			return base.CommandUserError
265: 		}
266: 
267: 		if err := c.SetupWorkerPublicAddress(c.Config, ""); err != nil {
268: 			c.UI.Error(err.Error())
269: 			return base.CommandUserError
270: 		}
271: 		c.InfoKeys = append(c.InfoKeys, "worker public proxy addr")
272: 		c.Info["worker public proxy addr"] = c.Config.Worker.PublicAddr
273: 
274: 		if c.Config.Controller != nil {
275: 			switch len(c.Config.Worker.Controllers) {
276: 			case 0:
277: 				if c.Config.Controller.PublicClusterAddr != "" {
278: 					clusterAddr = c.Config.Controller.PublicClusterAddr
279: 				}
280: 				c.Config.Worker.Controllers = []string{clusterAddr}
281: 			case 1:
282: 				if c.Config.Worker.Controllers[0] == clusterAddr {
283: 					break
284: 				}
285: 				if c.Config.Controller.PublicClusterAddr != "" &&
286: 					c.Config.Worker.Controllers[0] == c.Config.Controller.PublicClusterAddr {
287: 					break
288: 				}
289: 				// Best effort see if it's a domain name and if not assume it must match
290: 				host, _, err := net.SplitHostPort(c.Config.Worker.Controllers[0])
291: 				if err != nil && strings.Contains(err.Error(), "missing port in address") {
292: 					err = nil
293: 					host = c.Config.Worker.Controllers[0]
294: 				}
295: 				if err == nil {
296: 					ip := net.ParseIP(host)
297: 					if ip == nil {
298: 						// Assume it's a domain name
299: 						break
300: 					}
301: 				}
302: 				fallthrough
303: 			default:
304: 				c.UI.Error(`When running a combined controller and worker, it's invalid to specify a "controllers" key in the worker block with any value other than the controller cluster address/port when using IPs rather than DNS names`)
305: 				return base.CommandUserError
306: 			}
307: 		}
308: 		for _, controller := range c.Config.Worker.Controllers {
309: 			host, _, err := net.SplitHostPort(controller)
310: 			if err != nil {
311: 				if strings.Contains(err.Error(), "missing port in address") {
312: 					host = controller
313: 				} else {
314: 					c.UI.Error(fmt.Errorf("Invalid controller address %q: %w", controller, err).Error())
315: 					return base.CommandUserError
316: 				}
317: 			}
318: 			ip := net.ParseIP(host)
319: 			if ip != nil {
320: 				var errMsg string
321: 				switch {
322: 				case ip.IsUnspecified():
323: 					errMsg = "an unspecified"
324: 				case ip.IsMulticast():
325: 					errMsg = "a multicast"
326: 				}
327: 				if errMsg != "" {
328: 					c.UI.Error(fmt.Sprintf("Controller address %q is invalid: cannot be %s address", controller, errMsg))
329: 					return base.CommandUserError
330: 				}
331: 			}
332: 		}
333: 	}
334: 	if err := c.SetupListeners(c.UI, c.Config.SharedConfig, []string{"api", "cluster", "proxy", "ops"}); err != nil {
335: 		c.UI.Error(err.Error())
336: 		return base.CommandUserError
337: 	}
338: 
339: 	if c.Config.Controller != nil {
340: 		for _, ln := range c.Config.Listeners {
341: 			for _, purpose := range ln.Purpose {
342: 				if purpose != "cluster" {
343: 					continue
344: 				}
345: 				host, _, err := net.SplitHostPort(ln.Address)
346: 				if err != nil {
347: 					if strings.Contains(err.Error(), "missing port in address") {
348: 						host = ln.Address
349: 					} else {
350: 						c.UI.Error(fmt.Errorf("Invalid cluster listener address %q: %w", ln.Address, err).Error())
351: 						return base.CommandUserError
352: 					}
353: 				}
354: 				ip := net.ParseIP(host)
355: 				if ip != nil {
356: 					if ip.IsUnspecified() && c.Config.Controller.PublicClusterAddr == "" {
357: 						c.UI.Error(fmt.Sprintf("When %q listener has an unspecified address, %q must be set", "cluster", "public_cluster_addr"))
358: 						return base.CommandUserError
359: 					}
360: 				}
361: 			}
362: 		}
363: 	}
364: 
365: 	// Write out the PID to the file now that server has successfully started
366: 	if err := c.StorePidFile(c.Config.PidFile); err != nil {
367: 		c.UI.Error(fmt.Errorf("Error storing PID: %w", err).Error())
368: 		return base.CommandUserError
369: 	}
370: 
371: 	if c.Config.Controller != nil {
372: 		if c.Config.Controller.Database == nil || c.Config.Controller.Database.Url == "" {
373: 			c.UI.Error(`"url" not specified in "controller.database" config block"`)
374: 			return base.CommandUserError
375: 		}
376: 		var err error
377: 		c.DatabaseUrl, err = parseutil.ParsePath(c.Config.Controller.Database.Url)
378: 		if err != nil && !errors.Is(err, parseutil.ErrNotAUrl) {
379: 			c.UI.Error(fmt.Errorf("Error parsing database url: %w", err).Error())
380: 			return base.CommandUserError
381: 		}
382: 		c.DatabaseMaxOpenConnections = c.Config.Controller.Database.MaxOpenConnections
383: 
384: 		if err := c.ConnectToDatabase(c.Context, "postgres"); err != nil {
385: 			c.UI.Error(fmt.Errorf("Error connecting to database: %w", err).Error())
386: 			return base.CommandCliError
387: 		}
388: 
389: 		underlyingDB, err := c.Database.SqlDB(c.Context)
390: 		if err != nil {
391: 			c.UI.Error(fmt.Errorf("Can't get db: %w.", err).Error())
392: 			return base.CommandCliError
393: 		}
394: 		sMan, err := schema.NewManager(c.Context, "postgres", underlyingDB)
395: 		if err != nil {
396: 			c.UI.Error(fmt.Errorf("Can't get schema manager: %w.", err).Error())
397: 			return base.CommandCliError
398: 		}
399: 		// This is an advisory locks on the DB which is released when the db session ends.
400: 		if err := sMan.SharedLock(c.Context); err != nil {
401: 			c.UI.Error(fmt.Errorf("Unable to gain shared access to the database: %w", err).Error())
402: 			return base.CommandCliError
403: 		}
404: 		defer func() {
405: 			// The base context has already been canceled so we shouldn't use it here.
406: 			// 1 second is chosen so the shutdown is still responsive and this is a mostly
407: 			// non critical step since the lock should be released when the session with the
408: 			// database is closed.
409: 			ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 1*time.Second)
410: 			defer cancel()
411: 			if err := sMan.SharedUnlock(ctx); err != nil {
412: 				c.UI.Error(fmt.Errorf("Unable to release shared lock to the database: %w", err).Error())
413: 			}
414: 		}()
415: 		ckState, err := sMan.CurrentState(c.Context)
416: 		if err != nil {
417: 			c.UI.Error(fmt.Errorf("Error checking schema state: %w", err).Error())
418: 			return base.CommandCliError
419: 		}
420: 		if !ckState.Initialized {
421: 			c.UI.Error(base.WrapAtLength("The database has not been initialized. Please run 'boundary database init'."))
422: 			return base.CommandCliError
423: 		}
424: 		if !ckState.MigrationsApplied() {
425: 			for _, e := range ckState.Editions {
426: 				if e.DatabaseSchemaState == schema.Ahead {
427: 					c.UI.Error(base.WrapAtLength(fmt.Sprintf("Newer schema version (%s %d) "+
428: 						"than this binary expects. Please use a newer version of the boundary "+
429: 						"binary.", e.Name, e.DatabaseSchemaVersion)))
430: 					return base.CommandCliError
431: 				}
432: 			}
433: 			c.UI.Error(base.WrapAtLength("Database schema must be updated to use this version. Run 'boundary database migrate' to update the database. NOTE: Boundary does not currently support live migration; ensure all controllers are shut down before running the migration command."))
434: 			return base.CommandCliError
435: 		}
436: 		if err := c.verifyKmsSetup(); err != nil {
437: 			c.UI.Error(base.WrapAtLength("Database is in a bad state. Please revert the database into the last known good state."))
438: 			return base.CommandCliError
439: 		}
440: 	}
441: 
442: 	defer func() {
443: 		if err := c.RunShutdownFuncs(); err != nil {
444: 			c.UI.Error(fmt.Errorf("Error running shutdown tasks: %w", err).Error())
445: 		}
446: 	}()
447: 
448: 	if c.Config.Controller != nil {
449: 		c.EnabledPlugins = append(c.EnabledPlugins, base.EnabledPluginHostAws, base.EnabledPluginHostAzure)
450: 		if err := c.StartController(c.Context); err != nil {
451: 			c.UI.Error(err.Error())
452: 			return base.CommandCliError
453: 		}
454: 	}
455: 
456: 	if c.Config.Worker != nil {
457: 		if err := c.StartWorker(); err != nil {
458: 			c.UI.Error(err.Error())
459: 			if c.controller != nil {
460: 				if err := c.controller.Shutdown(); err != nil {
461: 					c.UI.Error(fmt.Errorf("Error with controller shutdown: %w", err).Error())
462: 				}
463: 			}
464: 			return base.CommandCliError
465: 		}
466: 		if c.WorkerAuthKms == nil {
467: 			req := c.worker.NodeeRegistrationRequest
468: 			if req == "" {
469: 				retErr := fmt.Errorf("No worker registration request found at worker startup time")
470: 				if err := c.worker.Shutdown(); err != nil {
471: 					c.UI.Error(retErr.Error())
472: 					retErr = fmt.Errorf("Error shutting down worker: %w", err)
473: 				}
474: 				c.UI.Error(retErr.Error())
475: 				if err := c.controller.Shutdown(); err != nil {
476: 					c.UI.Error(fmt.Errorf("Error with controller shutdown: %w", err).Error())
477: 				}
478: 				return base.CommandCliError
479: 			}
480: 			c.InfoKeys = append(c.InfoKeys, "worker registration request")
481: 			c.Info["worker registration request"] = req
482: 			c.InfoKeys = append(c.InfoKeys, "worker current key id")
483: 			c.Info["worker current key id"] = c.worker.NodeeCurrentKeyId
484: 		}
485: 	}
486: 
487: 	c.PrintInfo(c.UI)
488: 	if err := c.ReleaseLogGate(); err != nil {
489: 		c.UI.Error(fmt.Errorf("Error releasing event gate: %w", err).Error())
490: 		return base.CommandCliError
491: 	}
492: 
493: 	opsServer, err := ops.NewServer(c.Logger, c.controller, c.Listeners...)
494: 	if err != nil {
495: 		c.UI.Error(err.Error())
496: 		return base.CommandCliError
497: 	}
498: 	c.opsServer = opsServer
499: 	c.opsServer.Start()
500: 
501: 	// Inform any tests that the server is ready
502: 	if c.startedCh != nil {
503: 		close(c.startedCh)
504: 	}
505: 
506: 	return c.WaitForInterrupt()
507: }

509: func (c *Command) ParseFlagsAndConfig(args []string) int {
510: 	var err error
511: 
512: 	f := c.Flags()
513: 
514: 	if err = f.Parse(args); err != nil {
515: 		c.UI.Error(err.Error())
516: 		return base.CommandUserError
517: 	}
518: 
519: 	if len(c.flagConfig) == 0 && c.presetConfig == nil {
520: 		c.UI.Error("Must specify a config file using -config")
521: 		return base.CommandUserError
522: 	}
523: 
524: 	cfg, out := c.reloadConfig()
525: 	if out > 0 {
526: 		return out
527: 	}
528: 	c.Config = cfg
529: 
530: 	if c.Config.Controller == nil && c.Config.Worker == nil {
531: 		c.UI.Error("Neither worker nor controller specified in configuration file.")
532: 		return base.CommandUserError
533: 	}
534: 	if c.Config.Controller != nil && c.Config.Controller.Name == "" {
535: 		c.UI.Error("Controller has no name set. It must be the unique name of this instance.")
536: 		return base.CommandUserError
537: 	}
538: 	if c.Config.Worker != nil && c.Config.Worker.Name == "" {
539: 		c.UI.Error("Worker has no name set. It must be the unique name of this instance.")
540: 		return base.CommandUserError
541: 	}
542: 
543: 	return base.CommandSuccess
544: }
545: 
546: func (c *Command) reloadConfig() (*config.Config, int) {
547: 	const op = "server.(Command).reloadConfig"
548: 
549: 	var err error
550: 	var cfg *config.Config
551: 	switch {
552: 	case c.presetConfig != nil:
553: 		cfg, err = config.Parse(c.presetConfig.Load())
554: 
555: 	default:
556: 		wrapperPath := c.flagConfig
557: 		if c.flagConfigKms != "" {
558: 			wrapperPath = c.flagConfigKms
559: 		}
560: 		var configWrapper wrapping.Wrapper
561: 		var ifWrapper wrapping.InitFinalizer
562: 		var cleanupFunc func() error
563: 		if wrapperPath != "" {
564: 			configWrapper, cleanupFunc, err = wrapper.GetWrapperFromPath(
565: 				c.Context,
566: 				wrapperPath,
567: 				globals.KmsPurposeConfig,
568: 				configutil.WithPluginOptions(
569: 					pluginutil.WithPluginsMap(kms_plugin_assets.BuiltinKmsPlugins()),
570: 					pluginutil.WithPluginsFilesystem(kms_plugin_assets.KmsPluginPrefix, kms_plugin_assets.FileSystem()),
571: 				),
572: 				// TODO: How would we want to expose this kind of log to users when
573: 				// using recovery configs? Generally with normal CLI commands we
574: 				// don't print out all of these logs. We may want a logger with a
575: 				// custom writer behind our existing gate where we print nothing
576: 				// unless there is an error, then dump all of it.

663: func (c *Command) WaitForInterrupt() int {
664: 	const op = "server.(Command).WaitForInterrupt"
665: 	// Wait for shutdown
666: 	shutdownTriggered := false
667: 
668: 	for !shutdownTriggered {
669: 		select {
670: 		case <-c.ShutdownCh:
671: 			c.UI.Output("==> Boundary server shutdown triggered, interrupt again to force")
672: 
673: 			// Add a force-shutdown goroutine to consume another interrupt
674: 			abortForceShutdownCh := make(chan struct{})
675: 			defer close(abortForceShutdownCh)
676: 			go func() {
677: 				shutdownCh := make(chan os.Signal, 4)
678: 				signal.Notify(shutdownCh, os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)
679: 				select {
680: 				case <-shutdownCh:
681: 					c.UI.Error("Second interrupt received, forcing shutdown")
682: 					os.Exit(base.CommandUserError)
683: 				case <-abortForceShutdownCh:
684: 					// No-op, we just use this to shut down the goroutine
685: 				}
686: 			}()
687: 
688: 			if c.Config.Controller != nil {
689: 				c.opsServer.WaitIfHealthExists(c.Config.Controller.GracefulShutdownWaitDuration, c.UI)
690: 			}
691: 
692: 			// Do worker shutdown
693: 			if c.Config.Worker != nil {
694: 				if err := c.worker.Shutdown(); err != nil {
695: 					c.UI.Error(fmt.Errorf("Error shutting down worker: %w", err).Error())
696: 				}
697: 			}
698: 
699: 			// Do controller shutdown
700: 			if c.Config.Controller != nil {
701: 				if err := c.controller.Shutdown(); err != nil {
702: 					c.UI.Error(fmt.Errorf("Error shutting down controller: %w", err).Error())
703: 				}
704: 			}
705: 
706: 			err := c.opsServer.Shutdown()
707: 			if err != nil {
708: 				c.UI.Error(fmt.Errorf("Error shutting down ops listeners: %w", err).Error())
709: 			}
710: 
711: 			shutdownTriggered = true
712: 
713: 		case <-c.SighupCh:
714: 			c.UI.Output("==> Boundary server reload triggered")
715: 
716: 			// Check for new log level
717: 			var level hclog.Level
718: 			var newConf *config.Config
719: 			var out int
720: 
721: 			if c.flagConfig == "" && c.presetConfig == nil {
722: 				goto RUNRELOADFUNCS
723: 			}
724: 
725: 			newConf, out = c.reloadConfig()
726: 			if out > 0 {
727: 				goto RUNRELOADFUNCS
728: 			}
729: 
730: 			// Ensure at least one config was found.
731: 			if newConf == nil {
732: 				event.WriteError(context.TODO(), op, stderrors.New("no config found at reload time"))
733: 				goto RUNRELOADFUNCS
734: 			}
735: 
736: 			if newConf.LogLevel != "" {
737: 				configLogLevel := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(newConf.LogLevel))
738: 				switch configLogLevel {
739: 				case "trace":
740: 					level = hclog.Trace
741: 				case "debug":
742: 					level = hclog.Debug
743: 				case "notice", "info", "":
744: 					level = hclog.Info
745: 				case "warn", "warning":
746: 					level = hclog.Warn
747: 				case "err", "error":
748: 					level = hclog.Error
749: 				default:
750: 					event.WriteError(context.TODO(), op, stderrors.New("unknown log level found on reload"), event.WithInfo("level", newConf.LogLevel))
751: 					goto RUNRELOADFUNCS
752: 				}
753: 				c.Logger.SetLevel(level)
754: 			}
755: 
756: 		RUNRELOADFUNCS:
757: 			if err := c.Reload(newConf); err != nil {
758: 				c.UI.Error(fmt.Errorf("Error(s) were encountered during reload: %w", err).Error())
759: 			}
760: 
761: 		case <-c.SigUSR2Ch:
762: 			buf := make([]byte, 32*1024*1024)
763: 			n := runtime.Stack(buf[:], true)
764: 			event.WriteSysEvent(context.TODO(), op, "goroutine trace", "stack", string(buf[:n]))
765: 		}
766: 	}
767: 
768: 	return base.CommandSuccess
769: }
770: 
771: func (c *Command) Reload(newConf *config.Config) error {
772: 	c.ReloadFuncsLock.RLock()
773: 	defer c.ReloadFuncsLock.RUnlock()
774: 
775: 	var reloadErrors *multierror.Error
776: 
777: 	for _, relFunc := range c.ReloadFuncs["listeners"] {
778: 		if relFunc != nil {
779: 			if err := relFunc(); err != nil {
780: 				reloadErrors = multierror.Append(reloadErrors, fmt.Errorf("error encountered reloading listener: %w", err))
781: 			}
782: 		}
783: 	}
784: 
785: 	if newConf != nil && c.worker != nil {
786: 		c.worker.ParseAndStoreTags(newConf.Worker.Tags)
787: 	}
788: 
789: 	// Send a message that we reloaded. This prevents "guessing" sleep times
790: 	// in tests.
791: 	if c.reloadedCh != nil {
792: 		select {
793: 		case c.reloadedCh <- struct{}{}:
794: 		default:
795: 		}
796: 	}
797: 
798: 	return reloadErrors.ErrorOrNil()
799: }

36: func (w *Worker) startControllerConnections() error {
37: 	const op = "worker.(Worker).startControllerConnections"
38: 	initialAddrs := make([]resolver.Address, 0, len(w.conf.RawConfig.Worker.Controllers))
39: 	for _, addr := range w.conf.RawConfig.Worker.Controllers {
40: 		switch {
41: 		case strings.HasPrefix(addr, "/"):
42: 			initialAddrs = append(initialAddrs, resolver.Address{Addr: addr})
43: 		default:
44: 			host, port, err := net.SplitHostPort(addr)
45: 			if err != nil && strings.Contains(err.Error(), "missing port in address") {
46: 				host, port, err = net.SplitHostPort(net.JoinHostPort(addr, "9201"))
47: 			}
48: 			if err != nil {
49: 				return fmt.Errorf("error parsing controller address: %w", err)
50: 			}
51: 			initialAddrs = append(initialAddrs, resolver.Address{Addr: net.JoinHostPort(host, port)})
52: 		}
53: 	}
54: 
55: 	if len(initialAddrs) == 0 {
56: 		return errors.New("no initial controller addresses found")
57: 	}
58: 
59: 	w.Resolver().InitialState(resolver.State{
60: 		Addresses: initialAddrs,
61: 	})
62: 	if err := w.createClientConn(initialAddrs[0].Addr); err != nil {
63: 		return fmt.Errorf("error making client connection to controller: %w", err)
64: 	}
65: 
66: 	return nil

69: func (w *Worker) controllerDialerFunc() func(context.Context, string) (net.Conn, error) {
70: 	return func(ctx context.Context, addr string) (net.Conn, error) {
71: 		var conn net.Conn
72: 		var err error
73: 		switch {
74: 		case w.conf.WorkerAuthKms != nil:
75: 			conn, err = w.v1KmsAuthDialFn(ctx, addr)
76: 		default:
77: 			conn, err = protocol.Dial(ctx, w.NodeeFileStorage, addr, nodeenrollment.WithWrapper(w.conf.WorkerAuthStorageKms))
78: 		}
79: 
80: 		if !w.everAuthenticated.Load() && err == nil && conn != nil {
81: 			w.everAuthenticated.Store(true)
82: 		}
83: 
84: 		return conn, err
85: 	}
86: }

88: func (w *Worker) v1KmsAuthDialFn(ctx context.Context, addr string) (net.Conn, error) {
89: 	const op = "worker.(Worker).v1KmsAuthDialFn"
90: 	tlsConf, authInfo, err := w.workerAuthTLSConfig()
91: 	if err != nil {
92: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error creating tls config for worker auth: %w", err)
93: 	}
94: 	dialer := &net.Dialer{}
95: 	var nonTlsConn net.Conn
96: 	switch {
97: 	case strings.HasPrefix(addr, "/"):
98: 		nonTlsConn, err = dialer.DialContext(ctx, "unix", addr)
99: 	default:
100: 		nonTlsConn, err = dialer.DialContext(ctx, "tcp", addr)
101: 	}
102: 	if err != nil {
103: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unable to dial to controller: %w", err)
104: 	}
105: 	tlsConn := tls.Client(nonTlsConn, tlsConf)
106: 	written, err := tlsConn.Write([]byte(authInfo.ConnectionNonce))
107: 	if err != nil {
108: 		if err := nonTlsConn.Close(); err != nil {
109: 			event.WriteError(ctx, op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("error closing connection after writing failure"))
110: 		}
111: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unable to write connection nonce: %w", err)
112: 	}
113: 	if written != len(authInfo.ConnectionNonce) {
114: 		if err := nonTlsConn.Close(); err != nil {
115: 			event.WriteError(ctx, op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("error closing connection after writing failure"))
116: 		}
117: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("expected to write %d bytes of connection nonce, wrote %d", len(authInfo.ConnectionNonce), written)
118: 	}
119: 	return tlsConn, nil

122: func (w *Worker) createClientConn(addr string) error {
123: 	const op = "worker.(Worker).createClientConn"
124: 	defaultTimeout := (time.Second + time.Nanosecond).String()
125: 	defServiceConfig := fmt.Sprintf(`
126: 	  {
127: 		"loadBalancingConfig": [ { "round_robin": {} } ],
128: 		"methodConfig": [

130: 			"name": [],
131: 			"timeout": %q,
132: 			"waitForReady": true
133: 		  }
134: 		]

137: 	res := w.Resolver()
138: 	dialOpts := []grpc.DialOption{
139: 		grpc.WithResolvers(res),
140: 		grpc.WithUnaryInterceptor(metric.InstrumentClusterClient()),
141: 		grpc.WithDefaultCallOptions(grpc.MaxCallRecvMsgSize(math.MaxInt32)),
142: 		grpc.WithDefaultCallOptions(grpc.MaxCallSendMsgSize(math.MaxInt32)),
143: 		grpc.WithContextDialer(w.controllerDialerFunc()),
144: 		grpc.WithTransportCredentials(insecure.NewCredentials()),
145: 		grpc.WithDefaultServiceConfig(defServiceConfig),
146: 		// Don't have the resolver reach out for a service config from the
147: 		// resolver, use the one specified as default
148: 		grpc.WithDisableServiceConfig(),
149: 		grpc.WithConnectParams(grpc.ConnectParams{

152: 				Multiplier: 1.2,

155: 			},

172: func (w *Worker) workerAuthTLSConfig() (*tls.Config, *base.WorkerAuthInfo, error) {
173: 	var err error
174: 	info := &base.WorkerAuthInfo{
175: 		Name:        w.conf.RawConfig.Worker.Name,
176: 		Description: w.conf.RawConfig.Worker.Description,
177: 	}
178: 
179: 	info.ConnectionNonce, err = w.nonceFn(20)
180: 	if err != nil {
181: 		return nil, nil, err
182: 	}
183: 
184: 	pubKey, privKey, err := ed25519.GenerateKey(w.conf.SecureRandomReader)
185: 	if err != nil {
186: 		return nil, nil, err
187: 	}
188: 	host, err := base62.Random(20)
189: 	if err != nil {
190: 		return nil, nil, err
191: 	}
192: 
193: 	template := &x509.Certificate{
194: 		ExtKeyUsage: []x509.ExtKeyUsage{
195: 			x509.ExtKeyUsageServerAuth,
196: 			x509.ExtKeyUsageClientAuth,
197: 		},
198: 		DNSNames:              []string{host},
199: 		KeyUsage:              x509.KeyUsageDigitalSignature | x509.KeyUsageKeyEncipherment | x509.KeyUsageKeyAgreement | x509.KeyUsageCertSign,
200: 		SerialNumber:          big.NewInt(mathrand.Int63()),
201: 		NotBefore:             time.Now().Add(-30 * time.Second),
202: 		NotAfter:              time.Now().Add(globals.WorkerAuthNonceValidityPeriod),
203: 		BasicConstraintsValid: true,
204: 		IsCA:                  true,
205: 	}
206: 	certBytes, err := x509.CreateCertificate(w.conf.SecureRandomReader, template, template, pubKey, privKey)
207: 	if err != nil {
208: 		return nil, nil, err
209: 	}
210: 
211: 	certPEMBlock := &pem.Block{
212: 		Type:  "CERTIFICATE",
213: 		Bytes: certBytes,
214: 	}
215: 	info.CertPEM = pem.EncodeToMemory(certPEMBlock)
216: 
217: 	marshaledKey, err := x509.MarshalPKCS8PrivateKey(privKey)
218: 	if err != nil {
219: 		return nil, nil, err
220: 	}
221: 	keyPEMBlock := &pem.Block{
222: 		Type:  "PRIVATE KEY",
223: 		Bytes: marshaledKey,
224: 	}
225: 	info.KeyPEM = pem.EncodeToMemory(keyPEMBlock)
226: 
227: 	// Marshal and encrypt
228: 	marshaledInfo, err := json.Marshal(info)
229: 	if err != nil {
230: 		return nil, nil, err
231: 	}
232: 	encInfo, err := w.conf.WorkerAuthKms.Encrypt(w.baseContext, marshaledInfo)
233: 	if err != nil {
234: 		return nil, nil, err
235: 	}
236: 	marshaledEncInfo, err := proto.Marshal(encInfo)
237: 	if err != nil {
238: 		return nil, nil, err
239: 	}
240: 	b64alpn := base64.RawStdEncoding.EncodeToString(marshaledEncInfo)
241: 	var nextProtos []string
242: 	var count int
243: 	for i := 0; i < len(b64alpn); i += 230 {
244: 		end := i + 230
245: 		if end > len(b64alpn) {
246: 			end = len(b64alpn)
247: 		}
248: 		nextProtos = append(nextProtos, fmt.Sprintf("v1workerauth-%02d-%s", count, b64alpn[i:end]))
249: 		count++
250: 	}
251: 
252: 	cert, err := x509.ParseCertificate(certBytes)
253: 	if err != nil {
254: 		return nil, nil, err
255: 	}
256: 
257: 	// Build local tls config
258: 	rootCAs := x509.NewCertPool()
259: 	rootCAs.AddCert(cert)
260: 	tlsCert, err := tls.X509KeyPair(info.CertPEM, info.KeyPEM)
261: 	if err != nil {
262: 		return nil, nil, err
263: 	}
264: 	tlsConfig := &tls.Config{
265: 		ServerName:   host,
266: 		Certificates: []tls.Certificate{tlsCert},
267: 		RootCAs:      rootCAs,
268: 		NextProtos:   nextProtos,
269: 		MinVersion:   tls.VersionTLS13,
270: 	}
271: 
272: 	return tlsConfig, info, nil
273: }
